$comment: Ch 2 — THE SPIN, anchor scenario (Ray Allen 2013 Game 6 archetype). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7, voice uses moment + location ('GAME SIX. CORNER POCKET.'), never the athlete's name.

meta.title: GAME SIX. CORNER POCKET.
meta.subtitle: CH 2 — THE SPIN, ANCHOR
meta.authorIntent: The Game 6 corner three. d=6.7 (NBA corner distance = 22 ft), h_r=2.3, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=54°, v=8.67 m/s. Verification: t = 6.7 / (8.67·cos 54°) = 1.315 s; y(1.315) = 2.3 + 8.67·sin(54°)·1.315 − ½·9.8·1.315² = 3.051 m — matches h_h (err 0.001 m). Cultural framing is the moment ('GAME SIX. CORNER POCKET.'), not the athlete — per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7. Pedagogically this is where spin and pressure meet: full-court adrenaline, a clean release, and a rim that holds because the ball is rotating right.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. Legs from the corner had nothing left.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. Backspin only helps if the arc clears.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle higher.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Even with rotation, that's too much pace.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. Spin is forgiving, not a refund on power.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball from the corner. The line was lost.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=54°, v=8.67 m/s. The arc clears 4 m and lands cleanly through the cylinder — visibly the 'rope' shot the chapter is celebrating.

hints.body: Corner three: about 22 feet (6.7 m). Same equations, longer arc.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Distance jumps; velocity jumps with it.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 54°. Expect v in the high 8s.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 8.67 m/s.

intro.headline: GAME SIX. CORNER POCKET.
intro.subhead: Full-court legs. Right corner. The rotation is the whole shot.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Clean rotation under full pressure. Rope.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Kissed the corner of the square. In.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Back iron, the spin held it, the shot fell.

miss.headline: OFF THE IRON

diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Even with rotation, that's too much pace.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. Legs from the corner had nothing left.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. Spin is forgiving, not a refund on power.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball from the corner. The line was lost.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 54°, push v near 8.7.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 54°, cut v by ~0.4.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 54°, push v near 8.7.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 54°, cut v by ~0.8.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
